title: Severe Bleeding
summary: Apply immediate pressure and call emergency services for heavy bleeding
steps: Apply direct pressure with clean cloth over the wound (do not release)
If bleeding continues, apply a tourniquet above the wound (closer to the heart)
Record the time applied; loosen every 40-50 minutes for 1-2 minutes
Lay patient flat; elevate injured area above heart level
Keep patient warm and reassure until help arrives
warning: Never apply powders (like Yunnan Baiyao powder) directly into deep wounds — this can cause severe infection

title: Cardiac Arrest
summary: Begin CPR immediately if person is unconscious with no breathing or pulse
steps: Ensure scene safety, then tap shoulders and shout to check responsiveness
Watch chest/abdomen rise for 5-10 seconds while feeling for carotid pulse
If no breathing or only gasping, call for help and get an AED if available
Start chest compressions: center of chest, hands stacked, push 5-6 cm deep, 100-120 per minute
After 30 compressions, open airway and give 2 rescue breaths (compressions-only is OK if unable)
Continue CPR until AED arrives or EMS takes over
warning: CPR may cause rib fractures, but saving a life takes priority — don't worry about this

title: Choking (Airway Obstruction)
summary: Use Heimlich maneuver when foreign object blocks the airway completely
steps: Ask "Are you choking?" — if they nod and cannot speak, cough, or breathe
Stand behind victim, make a fist above navel (about two fingers width)
Cover fist with other hand, thrust inward and upward sharply
Repeat thrusts until object is expelled or victim loses consciousness
If unconscious, begin CPR immediately (check mouth for visible object when opening airway)
warning: For conscious victims who can still cough, encourage coughing — do not perform Heimlich maneuver yet
